title: Bayes' Theorem: Updating Beliefs
concept: Bayes' Theorem is a mathematical formula that updates the probability of a hypothesis as new evidence becomes available.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does the notation P(A | B) represent?

options.text: The probability of A happening, given that B has already happened
The probability of A and B happening at the exact same time
The probability of A divided by the probability of B

exercises.explain: The vertical line stands for 'given', so P(A | B) is the conditional probability of A given B.

cards.title: A positive medical test
cards.body: You test positive for a rare disease that only 0.1% of people have. The test is 99% accurate. Do you have a 99% chance of having the disease? Surprisingly, no. The actual chance is much lower.

cards.title: Bayes' Theorem
cards.body: Bayes' Theorem is a formula that calculates P(A | B) using P(B | A). It lets us update our initial belief (prior probability) in light of new evidence.

cards.title: The detective's clue
cards.body: Imagine a detective investigating a crime. They start with a list of suspects (prior beliefs). As they find clues (new evidence), they update the probability for each suspect. Bayes' Theorem is the math behind this update.

cards.title: The false positive paradox
cards.body: In our disease example, out of 10,000 people, only 10 actually have it. But with a 99% accurate test, about 100 healthy people will test positive by mistake. So if you test positive, you are one of 110 people, making your true risk only about 9%!

cards.title: Prior belief is not cheating
cards.body: Some think starting with a 'prior' belief is unscientific or biased. But in ML, ignoring prior context is a huge mistake. If a rare event triggers a warning, the prior probability keeps the AI from constantly crying wolf.

cards.title: Updating our beliefs
cards.body: How Bayes' Theorem updates our confidence:

left.text:
Initial estimate before evidence
e.g. Disease is very rare (0.1%)

right.text:
Updated estimate after evidence
e.g. Risk rises to 9% after positive test

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of Bayes' Theorem?

options.text: To update the probability of a hypothesis when new evidence arrives
To calculate the average of a symmetric bell curve
To find the median of a skewed dataset
To prove that all tests are 100% accurate

exercises.explain: Bayes' Theorem provides the mathematical framework for updating our beliefs (probabilities) based on new data or evidence.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The 'prior probability' is the probability of an event after new evidence is taken into account.
exercises.explain: False! The prior probability is our estimate before we see new evidence. The updated probability is called the 'posterior' probability.

exercises.sentence: In Bayes' Theorem, the initial probability estimate before seeing any new evidence is called the ___ probability.
exercises.explain: The prior probability represents what we know or believe before new evidence is introduced.

exercises.prompt: Why is a positive test for an extremely rare disease often not a cause for immediate panic?

options.text: Because the number of false positives among healthy people can easily dwarf the number of true positives.
Because medical tests are always completely random.
Because rare diseases actually do not exist.

exercises.explain: Since the disease is so rare, even a highly accurate test generates more total false positives from the large healthy population than true positives from the tiny sick population.

exercises.prompt: An ML email classifier starts with a prior probability that any email is 10% likely to be spam. It then scans an incoming email and finds the word 'Viagra'. The model calculates that the word 'Viagra' is common in spam but very rare in normal emails. What should the classifier do?

options.text: Use Bayes' Theorem to update the email's spam probability upward from the initial 10% prior.
Ignore the word 'Viagra' and stick to the 10% prior.
Instantly declare the email 100% spam without using any formula.
Set the spam probability back to 0%.

exercises.explain: Spot on! Bayes' Theorem allows the classifier to combine the prior probability (10%) with the new evidence (the presence of 'Viagra') to calculate a much higher, updated spam probability. Congratulations on completing Unit 5!
